title: Connector manifest (L4 binding)
description: A concrete binding from a SaaS/cloud product to the evidence types it can emit. Each manifest declares the connector identity, supported evidence types (from the 12-kind union: policy, procedure, log, screenshot, report, attestation, configuration, training_record, incident_record, fria, dpia, contract), an auth shape, and a list of probes the runtime engine can fire. Manifests are illustrative — verify against vendor docs before wiring up a production connector.

connector_id.description: Stable kebab-case identifier for the connector, e.g. 'okta', 'azure-ad', 'github'.

evidence_types_emitted.description: Subset of the 12-kind EvidenceType union this connector can emit.

auth.description: How the engine authenticates against the connector.

scopes_required.description: Scopes/permissions the engine requests. Names are connector-specific.

probes.description: List of probe definitions the runtime can fire to collect evidence.

probe_id.description: Stable kebab-case identifier for the probe.

satisfies_controls_hint.description: Non-binding hint: control IDs this probe may help satisfy. Engines should use the framework registry as the source of truth.

notes.description: Free-form notes. Manifests are synthetic/illustrative; verify against vendor docs before use.
